title: Venezuelan Chicken Empanada con Maracuya
description: A rich, dense, and flaky pastry filled with tender chicken, caramelized onions, and sweet marshmallows, inspired by the flavors of Venezuela.

ingredients.name: Caramelized onions
ingredients.notes: Use a mixture of yellow and red onions for added depth

ingredients.name: Miniature marshmallows
ingredients.notes: Use a low-sugar or sugar-free marshmallow for keto-friendliness

ingredients.name: Chopped cilantro
ingredients.notes: Fresh cilantro adds a bright and fresh flavor

ingredients.name: Garlic powder
ingredients.notes: Use a small amount to avoid overpowering the dish

ingredients.name: Ground cumin
ingredients.notes: Adds a warm and earthy flavor

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use a flaky sea salt for added texture

ingredients.name: Large egg
ingredients.notes: Use for egg wash and pastry

ingredients.name: Venezuelan-style pastry dough (or gluten-free equivalent)
ingredients.notes: Make sure to store in an airtight container in the refrigerator

instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large skillet, cook the ground chicken over medium-high heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the caramelized onions, garlic powder, cumin, and salt to the skillet and stir to combine.
Stir in the miniature marshmallows and cook until they start to puff and brown.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl and stir in the chopped cilantro.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the pastry dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch (3mm).
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon or two of the chicken mixture onto one half of each dough circle, leaving a 1/2 inch (1cm) border around the edges.
Brush the edges with the beaten egg and fold the dough in half to form a triangle or a crescent shape.
Press the edges together with a fork to seal the empanada and use a knife to cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little bit of vegan butter or shortening and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
